progress_from_term_ind.solution:
Theorem progress' : forall t T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  value t \/ exists t', t --> t'.
Proof.
  intros t.
  induction t; intros T Ht; auto.
  - (* var *) inversion Ht. subst. inversion H1.
  - (* app *) right.
    inversion Ht; subst.
    destruct (IHt1 _ H2) as [Hv1 | [t1' Hs1]].
    + destruct (IHt2 _ H4) as [Hv2 | [t2' Hs2]].
      * inversion Ht; subst. inversion Hv1; subst.
        -- exists <{ [x0 := t2] t0 }>. apply ST_AppAbs. assumption.
        -- inversion H2.
        -- inversion H2.
      * exists <{ t1 t2' }>. apply ST_App2; assumption.
    + exists <{ t1' t2 }>. apply ST_App1. assumption.
  - (* if *) right.
    inversion Ht; subst.
    destruct (IHt1 _ H3) as [Hv1 | [t1' Hs1]].
    + inversion Hv1; subst.
      * exists t2. apply ST_IfTrue.
      * exists t3. apply ST_IfFalse.
      * inversion H3.
    + exists <{ if t1' then t2 else t3 }>. apply ST_If. assumption.
Qed.
progress_from_term_ind.explanation: Progress by induction on the term rather than the typing derivation. Variables in empty context are contradictory.

substitution_preserves_typing_from_typin.solution:
Theorem substitution_preserves_typing' : forall Gamma x U t v T,
  (x |-> U ; Gamma) |-- t \in T ->
  empty |-- v \in U ->
  Gamma |-- [x:=v]t \in T.
Proof.
  intros Gamma x U t v T Ht Hv.
  generalize dependent Gamma. generalize dependent T.
  induction t; intros T Gamma Ht;
    inversion Ht; subst; simpl; eauto.
  - (* var *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s).
    + subst. rewrite update_eq in H1. injection H1 as H1. subst.
      apply weakening_empty. assumption.
    + apply T_Var. rewrite update_neq in H1; assumption.
  - (* abs *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s).
    + subst. apply T_Abs. rewrite update_shadow in H4. assumption.
    + apply T_Abs. apply IHt. rewrite update_permute; auto.
Qed.
substitution_preserves_typing_from_typin.explanation: Substitution preserves typing, proved by induction on the term. Key cases: variable lookup, and lambda where we handle shadowing.

subject_expansion_stlc.solution:
(* Subject expansion does NOT hold for STLC. *)
(* Counterexample: (\x:Bool. true) false --> true : Bool,
   but (\x:Bool. true) false is also well-typed.
   A real counterexample: if true then (\x:Bool.x) else true --> \x:Bool.x
   The result has type Bool->Bool but the original term would need both branches to agree.
   Actually, consider: (\x:Bool. true) (if true then true else (\y:Bool.y))
   This steps to (\x:Bool. true) true (well-typed) but the original has an ill-typed if. *)
subject_expansion_stlc.explanation: Subject expansion fails in STLC because a well-typed reduct may have originated from an ill-typed term, especially with if-expressions having mismatched branch types.

type_soundness.solution:
Theorem type_soundness : forall t t' T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  t -->* t' ->
  ~(stuck t').
Proof.
  intros t t' T HT P [Hnf Hnv].
  induction P.
  - apply progress in HT. destruct HT; auto.
  - apply IHP.
    + eapply preservation; eassumption.
    + split; assumption.
Qed.
type_soundness.explanation: Type soundness: well-typed terms never get stuck. Follows from progress and preservation by induction on multi-step.

unique_types.solution:
Theorem unique_types : forall Gamma e T T',
  Gamma |-- e \in T ->
  Gamma |-- e \in T' ->
  T = T'.
Proof.
  intros Gamma e T T' HT HT'.
  generalize dependent T'.
  induction HT; intros T' HT'; inversion HT'; subst.
  - (* var *) rewrite H1 in H3. injection H3. auto.
  - (* abs *) f_equal. apply IHHT. assumption.
  - (* app *) apply IHHT1 in H2. injection H2. auto.
  - (* true *) reflexivity.
  - (* false *) reflexivity.
  - (* if *) apply IHHT2. assumption.
Qed.
unique_types.explanation: Types are unique in STLC: by induction on the first typing derivation, inverting the second. Each case uses the IH to match types.

afi.solution:
(* Manual grade exercise: list all places where appears_free_in is used.
   The afi constructors are:
   - afi_var: appears_free_in x (tm_var x)
   - afi_app1: appears_free_in x t1 -> appears_free_in x (t1 t2)
   - afi_app2: appears_free_in x t2 -> appears_free_in x (t1 t2)
   - afi_abs: x <> y -> appears_free_in x t -> appears_free_in x (\y:T, t)
   - afi_if1, afi_if2, afi_if3: for if branches *)
afi.explanation: The appears_free_in relation tracks where variables occur free: in the variable itself, either side of an application, under a lambda (if not shadowed), and in if branches.

progress_preservation_statement.solution:
(* Manual grade exercise.
   Progress: forall t T, empty |-- t : T -> value t \/ exists t', t --> t'.
   Preservation: forall t t' T, empty |-- t : T -> t --> t' -> empty |-- t' : T.
   These are the standard safety properties for STLC. *)
progress_preservation_statement.explanation: State the progress and preservation theorems for STLC, showing that well-typed programs don't get stuck.

free_in_context.solution:
Lemma free_in_context : forall x t T Gamma,
  appears_free_in x t ->
  Gamma |-- t \in T ->
  exists T', Gamma x = Some T'.
Proof.
  intros x t T Gamma Hafi Htyp.
  induction Htyp; inversion Hafi; subst.
  - (* T_Var *) exists T. assumption.
  - (* T_Abs *) apply IHHtyp in H4. destruct H4 as [T' HT'].
    exists T'. rewrite update_neq in HT'; assumption.
  - (* T_App left *) apply IHHtyp1. assumption.
  - (* T_App right *) apply IHHtyp2. assumption.
  - (* T_If *) apply IHHtyp1. assumption.
  - (* T_If *) apply IHHtyp2. assumption.
  - (* T_If *) apply IHHtyp3. assumption.
Qed.
free_in_context.explanation: If x appears free in t, and Gamma types t, then x must be in Gamma. By induction on the typing derivation, following the free occurrence.

typable_empty__closed.solution:
Corollary typable_empty__closed : forall t T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  closed t.
Proof.
  unfold closed, not. intros t T HT x Hfree.
  apply free_in_context with (T := T) (Gamma := empty) in Hfree; [| assumption].
  destruct Hfree as [T' HT']. discriminate.
Qed.
typable_empty__closed.explanation: If t is typable in the empty context, then t has no free variables, because any free variable would need to be in the empty context.

context_invariance.solution:
Lemma context_invariance : forall Gamma Gamma' t T,
  Gamma |-- t \in T ->
  (forall x, appears_free_in x t -> Gamma x = Gamma' x) ->
  Gamma' |-- t \in T.
Proof.
  intros Gamma Gamma' t T HT Heq.
  generalize dependent Gamma'.
  induction HT; intros Gamma' Heq.
  - (* T_Var *) apply T_Var. rewrite <- Heq; [assumption | constructor].
  - (* T_Abs *) apply T_Abs. apply IHHT. intros y Hy.
    destruct (String.eqb_spec x y).
    + subst. rewrite update_eq. rewrite update_eq. reflexivity.
    + rewrite update_neq; [| assumption]. rewrite update_neq; [| assumption].
      apply Heq. apply afi_abs; assumption.
  - (* T_App *) eapply T_App.
    + apply IHHT1. intros x Hx. apply Heq. apply afi_app1. assumption.
    + apply IHHT2. intros x Hx. apply Heq. apply afi_app2. assumption.
  - (* T_True *) apply T_True.
  - (* T_False *) apply T_False.
  - (* T_If *) apply T_If.
    + apply IHHT1. intros x Hx. apply Heq. apply afi_if1. assumption.
    + apply IHHT2. intros x Hx. apply Heq. apply afi_if2. assumption.
    + apply IHHT3. intros x Hx. apply Heq. apply afi_if3. assumption.
Qed.
context_invariance.explanation: If two contexts agree on all free variables of t, they assign the same type. By induction on the typing derivation.

stlc_variation1.solution:
(* Adding T_Var_Wrong: |-- true \in Bool -> Bool *)
(* Determinism: remains true (typing doesn't affect step) *)
(* Progress: remains true *)
(* Preservation: becomes false *)
stlc_variation1.explanation: With T_Var_Wrong, we could type true as Bool->Bool, then true true would be well-typed but stuck.

stlc_variation2.solution:
(* Removing T_App rule *)
(* Determinism: remains true *)
(* Progress: remains true (vacuously - fewer typed terms) *)
(* Preservation: remains true *)
stlc_variation2.explanation: Without T_App, applications are never well-typed, so progress and preservation hold vacuously for applications.

stlc_variation3.solution:
(* Removing ST_App1 rule *)
(* Determinism: remains true *)
(* Progress: becomes false *)
(* Preservation: remains true *)
stlc_variation3.explanation: Without ST_App1, terms like (\x.x) (\x.x) true can't reduce the function position, breaking progress.

stlc_variation4.solution:
(* Removing the condition that t1 be a value in ST_App2 *)
(* Determinism: becomes false *)
(* Progress: remains true *)
(* Preservation: remains true *)
stlc_variation4.explanation: Without the value restriction on ST_App2, both ST_App1 and ST_App2 could apply, breaking determinism.

stlc_variation5.solution:
(* Adding call-by-name: (\x:T,t) t2 --> [x:=t2]t without requiring t2 to be a value *)
(* Determinism: becomes false *)
(* Progress: remains true *)
(* Preservation: remains true (if substitution lemma still holds for non-values) *)
stlc_variation5.explanation: Call-by-name conflicts with call-by-value ST_App2, breaking determinism.

stlc_variation6.solution:
(* Adding evaluation under lambda: t1 --> t1' => \x:T, t1 --> \x:T, t1' *)
(* Determinism: remains true *)
(* Progress: remains true *)
(* Preservation: remains true *)
stlc_variation6.explanation: Evaluating under lambda doesn't conflict with other rules and preserves types.

stlc_variation7.solution:
(* Adding fix-point rule *)
(* Determinism: remains true *)
(* Progress: remains true *)
(* Preservation: depends on typing of fix *)
stlc_variation7.explanation: A fix-point combinator adds recursion. If properly typed, preservation holds.

STLCArith_subst.solution:
(* Extended STLC with arithmetic: define substitution for the additional cases *)
Fixpoint subst (x : string) (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tm_var y => if String.eqb x y then s else t
  | <{\y:T, t1}> => if String.eqb x y then t else <{\y:T, [x:=s] t1}>
  | <{t1 t2}> => <{([x:=s] t1) ([x:=s] t2)}>
  | <{true}> => <{true}>
  | <{false}> => <{false}>
  | <{if t1 then t2 else t3}> => <{if [x:=s]t1 then [x:=s]t2 else [x:=s]t3}>
  | <{0}> => <{0}>
  | <{succ t1}> => <{succ ([x:=s] t1)}>
  | <{pred t1}> => <{pred ([x:=s] t1)}>
  | <{t1 * t2}> => <{([x:=s] t1) * ([x:=s] t2)}>
  | <{if0 t1 then t2 else t3}> => <{if0 [x:=s]t1 then [x:=s]t2 else [x:=s]t3}>
  end.
STLCArith_subst.explanation: Extend substitution to handle nat constants, succ, pred, multiplication, and if0.

STLCArith_weakening.solution:
Lemma weakening : forall Gamma Gamma' t T,
  includedin Gamma Gamma' ->
  Gamma |-- t \in T ->
  Gamma' |-- t \in T.
Proof.
  intros Gamma Gamma' t T Hinc HT.
  generalize dependent Gamma'.
  induction HT; intros Gamma' Hinc; eauto.
  - apply T_Var. apply Hinc. assumption.
  - apply T_Abs. apply IHHT. apply includedin_update. assumption.
Qed.
STLCArith_weakening.explanation: Weakening for STLCArith: if Gamma is included in Gamma', typing is preserved. By induction on the typing derivation.

STLCArith_preservation.solution:
Theorem preservation : forall t t' T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  t --> t' ->
  empty |-- t' \in T.
Proof with eauto.
  intros t t' T HT HE.
  generalize dependent t'.
  induction HT; intros t' HE; try solve_by_invert;
    inversion HE; subst...
  - (* ST_AppAbs *)
    inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
Qed.
STLCArith_preservation.explanation: Preservation for STLCArith: by induction on typing, inverting the step. Beta reduction uses the substitution lemma.

STLCArith_progress.solution:
Theorem progress : forall t T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  value t \/ (exists t', t --> t').
Proof with eauto.
  intros t T Ht.
  remember empty as Gamma.
  induction Ht; subst...
  - (* var *) inversion H.
  - (* app *) right.
    destruct IHHt1 as [Hv1 | [t1' Hs1]]...
    destruct IHHt2 as [Hv2 | [t2' Hs2]]...
    + inversion Hv1; subst; try solve_by_invert.
      exists <{ [x0:=t2]t0 }>...
    + exists <{ t1 t2' }>...
  - (* if *) right.
    destruct IHHt1 as [Hv1 | [t1' Hs1]]...
    + inversion Hv1; subst; try solve_by_invert...
    + exists <{ if t1' then t2 else t3 }>...
Qed.
STLCArith_progress.explanation: Progress for STLCArith: well-typed terms in the empty context are either values or can step.
